user_message: Need help with Consolidated Messenger -- pull the context. The CSA flagged this as at-risk yesterday.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Sarah Singh <sarah.singh@consolidated.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-24 14:06 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Consolidated Messenger territory planning

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with territory planning. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
To: Sarah Singh <sarah.singh@consolidated.com>
Date: 2024-02-15 16:43 EST
Subject: URGENT: territory planning Issue at Consolidated Messenger

Our team encountered some challenges with territory planning. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

---
From: Sarah Singh <sarah.singh@consolidated.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-03 12:00 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Consolidated Messenger territory planning

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about territory planning. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

Meeting: territory planning Discussion - Consolidated Messenger
Date: 2024-02-04
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Sarah Singh (Consolidated Messenger), Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft), Priya (Consolidated Messenger)

[00:01:30] Sarah Singh (Consolidated Messenger): Thanks everyone for joining. Let's dive into territory planning.

[00:07:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): On the territory planning front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around vendor dependencies.

[00:12:00] Priya (Consolidated Messenger): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is technical validation.

[00:16:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on resource allocation and found that there's a compliance requirement we hadn't accounted for in the original scope.

[00:21:00] Priya (Consolidated Messenger): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about partner engagement. Can we address those?

[00:24:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): On the territory planning front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around integration complexity.

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF11-M trap (Brief format doesn't natively differentiate first-meeting vs recurring); produces complete One-page brief with attendee context, account snapshot, talking points, likely objections, open item
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF11-M failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF11-M failure mode (Brief format doesn't natively differentiate first-meeting vs recurring)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct territory planning decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF11-M -- Brief format doesn't natively differentiate first-meeting vs recurring
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
